title: Linting misses continuous assignments to registers
body:
---


Author Name: **Jeremy Bennett** (@jeremybennett)
Original Redmine Issue: 61 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2009-01-26


---

I tried breaking some @UNOPTFLAT@ loops by taking some blocking assignments out of combinatorial @always@ blocks and setting them by continuous assignment. This satisfied verilator.

Before:

```
always @(sprs_op or spr_addr or sys_data or spr_dat_mac or spr_dat_pic or spr_dat_pm or
	spr_dat_dmmu or spr_dat_immu or spr_dat_du or spr_dat_tt) begin
	case (sprs_op)	// synopsys parallel_case
		`OR1200_ALUOP_MTSR : begin
			write_spr = 1'b1;
			read_spr = 1'b0;
			to_wbmux = 32'b0;
		end
		`OR1200_ALUOP_MFSR :
...

```

After:

```
assign read_spr  = (sprs_op == `OR1200_ALUOP_MTSR ? 1'b0 :
		    sprs_op == `OR1200_ALUOP_MFSR ? 1'b1 : 1'b0);
assign write_spr = (sprs_op == `OR1200_ALUOP_MTSR ? 1'b1 :
		    sprs_op == `OR1200_ALUOP_MFSR ? 1'b0 : 1'b0);
    
always @(sprs_op or spr_addr or sys_data or spr_dat_mac or spr_dat_pic or spr_dat_pm or
	spr_dat_dmmu or spr_dat_immu or spr_dat_du or spr_dat_tt) begin
	case (sprs_op)	// synopsys parallel_case
		`OR1200_ALUOP_MTSR : begin
			to_wbmux = 32'b0;
		end
		`OR1200_ALUOP_MFSR : begin

```

As a check, I reran my modified code through an event driven simulator (Icarus verilog). It barfed because I had not realized the entities concerned (@read_spr@ and @write_spr@) were registers, not wires, and you can't continuously assign to a register.

It would be nice if Verilator would pick up this error, along with all its other detailed linting.

Not a problem for me, but added here for the record.
